tipRules.tip: Texas rig Senko with 3/0 or 4/0 hook and 1/8 or 3/16oz screw-on weight for flipping and pitching to bushes, brush piles, and matted vegetation
Senko on 3/16oz jighead is great finesse tactic for pressured waters or postspawn. Let it fall next to dock, shake in place, then retrieve
Scale down to 1/8oz jighead for suspended fish. Count down to right depth, then shake and reel back
Carolina rig Senko produces best in prespawn and postspawn when bass stage in front of spawning banks 6-10ft deep. Use 1oz tungsten weight with 4ft leader of 12lb fluorocarbon
3-inch Senko on 2/0 straight shank hook drop shot with 1/4 to 3/16oz weight 1ft below lure. Drop to depth of baitfish balls on electronics and shake to trigger strikes along bluffs and ledges

knowledge.insight: Most effective wacky style retrieve mimics crippled shad - raise and lower stick worm a couple feet, never letting it touch bottom. Pop it a couple times for herky-jerky action then return to subtle fall
Wacky rig causes Senko to fall sideways, gliding under pier platforms or foam flotation of docks where bass lurk in shadows. Especially effective when bass suspend under docks
Senko on jighead can be fished from 6 inches to 30-40ft of water. Vary jighead size to control depth presentation
